candidates.audit_note: PDF page 112: First Schedule heading reads '[Subsection 2(1)]'. Section 2(1) (page 13) is the Interpretation subsection that defines terms including categories of 'employee' the Schedule qualifies. Target correctly matches.

PDF page 112: 'Notwithstanding paragraph 1, the person whose wages exceeds four thousand ringgit...' refers to item '1.' in the same First Schedule table. The graph's provision-ID schema has no paragraph-level node for Schedule items, so this genuinely cannot be resolved to a specific provision ID. Correctly left unresolved.

PDF page 112: First Schedule item 1A lists 'Subsections 60(3), 60A(3), 60C(2A), 60D(3) and 60D(4) and section 60J' as the 'Provision of the Act not applicable' column. All six cited provisions are unambiguous and independently verifiable elsewhere in the Act. The graph schema supports multi-element target_provision_ids (used elsewhere), so this should have resolved to six targets rather than being left empty.
PDF page 13: '(1) This Act may be cited as the Employment Act 1955.' Clear self-reference to the whole Act.
PDF page 13: '(2) This Act shall apply to *Peninsular Malaysia only.' Clear self-reference to the whole Act.
PDF page 104: '...pays wages less than the rate provided under section 60 commits an offence...' Section 100(1) correctly cross-references section 60 (rest day pay).
PDF page 105 (subsection 100(1) continues across the page break): '...at the rate provided under section 60...' Second occurrence of the same valid cross-reference within subsection (1).
PDF page 105: '(2) Any employer who fails to pay...any overtime wages as provided under this Act...' Self-reference to the whole Act.
PDF page 105: '(3) Any employer who fails to pay...wages as provided under section 60D, commits an offence...' Correct cross-reference.
PDF page 105: second occurrence of 'section 60D' within subsection 100(3) text. Correct cross-reference.
PDF page 105: '(4) Any employer who fails to grant...annual leave...as provided under section 60E commits an offence...' Correct cross-reference.
PDF page 105: '(5) Any employer who fails to grant sick leave...as provided under section 60F commits an offence...' Correct cross-reference.
PDF page 105: second occurrence of 'section 60F' within subsection 100(5) text. Correct cross-reference.
PDF page 106: 'any officer lawfully exercising the powers of the Director General under this Act...' (section 101). Self-reference to the whole Act.

PDF page 106: section 101A(1) — 'compound any offence committed by a person which is punishable under this Act or any regulation made under this Act.' First of two 'this Act' occurrences in subsection (1).

PDF page 106: '(3) An offer under subsection (2) may be made at any time after the offence has been committed...' Correctly targets 101A(2).

PDF page 107: '(5) Any moneys paid...pursuant to subsection (2)...' Correctly targets 101A(2).

PDF page 108: '(2) For the purpose of subsection (1), it shall be presumed...' Correctly targets 101C(1).
PDF page 109: '(3) The first-mentioned person in subsection (2) includes the agent, manager or factor...' Correctly targets 101C(2).
PDF page 109: '102.(1) The Minister may...make such regulations...for giving full effect to the provisions of this Act...' First of two 'this Act' occurrences.
PDF page 109: second 'this Act' occurrence in 102(1).
